_schema.description: Manifest for the City of Mukilteo public photo collection. This file describes only kept, in-repo, web-optimized photo derivatives — it is a published-derivative catalog, not a full source inventory. Skipped/deferred source files are tracked outside this manifest in a local-only source map. Each entry includes originalFilename, originalSizeBytes, and originalSha256 for audit / cross-reference without exposing source folder paths.

fields.name: in-repo filename within photos/
fields.originalFilename: original filename only (no folder/contributor path) — for audit / cross-reference
fields.originalSha256: SHA-256 of the original source file — enables tamper detection and content-based reverse lookup
fields.category: historical | civic-building | park | waterfront | transportation | business | residential | broll | civic-event | infrastructure | public-art | historical-marker | unknown
fields.publicCaption: short caption suitable for public web use; empty for b-roll
fields.needsReview: true if a human should look at the image before publishing
fields.reviewFlags: specific reasons review is needed; subset of: location-unconfirmed, era-estimated, subject-identification-unconfirmed, caption-needed, contains-personal-name, multiple-similar-shots, possible-duplicate
fields.usage: confirmed uses; subset of: public-website, background, historical-archive
fields.candidateUses: speculative uses requiring visual confirmation; subset of: hero-image
fields.webSizeBytes: file size of the in-repo web-optimized JPG
fields.originalSizeBytes: byte size of the source original; useful for tracking how much optimization saved
fields.identifiablePeopleInFrame: true if recognizable individuals appear in the frame; image releases may be required before public use

entries.name: mukilteo-pier-lighthouse-park.jpg
entries.description: Mukilteo Lighthouse Park photographed from offshore at low tide. The Mukilteo Lighthouse and keeper's quarters sit center-right behind a low rip-rap shoreline; a wooden public pier on weathered pilings extends from a craftsman-style house at left. Conifer-covered bluffs rise in the background.
entries.publicCaption: Mukilteo Lighthouse and the public pier at Lighthouse Park.

entries.name: mukilteo-washington-banner.jpg
entries.description: Pre-rendered horizontal web banner: 'CITY OF MUKILTEO' wordmark and circular seal on a navy background at left, composited with a photograph of the Mukilteo Lighthouse and keeper's quarters at right.
entries.notes: Designed web banner (logo + photo composite). The composite uses a photo of the Mukilteo Lighthouse, but the asset itself is a graphic. Filename 'MukilteoWashington.jpg' suggests this was an older site header — confirm whether it's still in use before keeping in active assets.

entries.name: 92nd-street-park-yellow-flowers-archway.jpg
entries.description: B-roll: yellow flowers in focus with 92nd Street Park main archway behind.
entries.notes: Decorative; useful as page texture, not a documentary image. Near-duplicate of 92nd-street-park-5229.jpg in the same batch (same composition, this version is better resolved). Recommend choosing one as canonical b-roll.

entries.name: city-hall-broll-flowers.jpg
entries.description: B-roll: a single sharp serrated leaf in the foreground with the Mukilteo City Hall building softly blurred in the background.

entries.name: japanese-gulch-purple-flowers-trail.jpg
entries.description: Close-up of magenta foxglove (Digitalis) flower spikes in bloom, with a small wooden pavilion or shelter and a kiosk-style sign visible in the soft-focus background, set on a manicured lawn beside an asphalt path.
entries.notes: Background shows a manicured lawn with a pavilion/shelter and information kiosk — much more consistent with a community garden or developed park entrance than the Japanese Gulch trail. Strong visual similarity to 'community-garden.jpg' from the pilot batch — likely the same location. Original filename '125.JPG' is generic so the 'Japanese Gulch' label appears to be a renaming guess. Recommend renaming the file.

entries.name: 92nd-street-park-5224.jpg
entries.description: Wide paved walking path curving through a grass lawn at 92nd Street Park, with a small playground, picnic tables, and a shelter visible in the middle distance against a backdrop of mature evergreens.
entries.publicCaption: A walking path winds through 92nd Street Park.
entries.notes: Numeric suffix preserved for disambiguation.

entries.name: 92nd-street-park-5227.jpg
entries.description: Low-angle view of the wooden post-and-beam pavilion/archway at 92nd Street Park, with open lattice gable and surrounding evergreens against an overcast sky.
entries.publicCaption: The wooden archway at 92nd Street Park.

entries.name: 92nd-street-park-5229.jpg
entries.description: Yellow wildflowers in sharp foreground focus with the wooden post-and-beam archway at 92nd Street Park visible in the soft-focus background.

entries.name: 92nd-street-park-5233.jpg
entries.description: Carved wooden entrance sign for 92nd Street Park, with the park name in green relief lettering and a painted cartouche depicting the Mukilteo lighthouse and a sailboat. Flowering shrubs frame the base of the sign.
entries.publicCaption: 92nd Street Park.

entries.name: aid-unit.jpg
entries.description: Side-panel detail of Mukilteo Fire aid unit M-25, showing the gold 'MUKILTEO FIRE' lettering, unit number, and Star of Life EMS symbol.
entries.publicCaption: Mukilteo Fire Department aid unit.

entries.name: almgrens-landing-sign.jpg
entries.description: Hanging wooden sign at 601, reading 'ALMGREN'S LANDING' on green placards with a painted depiction of a small white-and-red boat at center.
entries.publicCaption: Almgren's Landing.

entries.name: arbors-gated-community.jpg
entries.description: Curving residential street through a planned multi-family neighborhood, with a wrought-iron lamp post and landscaped traffic island in the foreground and beige two- and three-story townhomes on either side under dramatic cumulus clouds.

entries.name: lighthouse-park-band-shell.jpg
entries.description: Heavy-timber park pavilion at Lighthouse Park with a covered central gathering area flanked by enclosed bays, photographed in soft evening light. Functions as both performance/event space and includes restroom facilities.
entries.publicCaption: Band shell at Lighthouse Park.

entries.name: barbara-brennan-park-sign.jpg

location.name: Barbara Brennen Dobra Memorial Park, Mukilteo

entries.description: Carved wooden sign for Barbara Brennen Dobra Memorial Park, with a painted lighthouse cartouche at top and the memorial name in cream relief lettering. Red dahlia-like flowers in foreground partially obscure additional inscription text.
entries.publicCaption: Barbara Brennen Dobra Memorial Park.

entries.name: lighthouse-park-beach-facing-south.jpg
entries.description: Pebble-and-driftwood beach at Lighthouse Park looking south down the Mukilteo waterfront, Puget Sound rolling in under a hazy sky.
entries.publicCaption: Mukilteo waterfront, looking south from Lighthouse Park.

entries.name: big-gulch-trailhead-archway.jpg
entries.description: Wide symmetrical view of the cedar timber-frame arch at the Big Gulch Trail North Fork trailhead, framing a gravel path into dense forest. Trailhead signboard with the City of Mukilteo logo mounted on the crossbar.
entries.publicCaption: The North Fork trailhead at Big Gulch Trail.

entries.name: big-gulch-trailhead-arch-closeup.jpg
entries.description: Close-up of the trailhead signboard mounted under the Big Gulch Trail arch, reading 'BIG GULCH TRAIL — NORTH FORK TRAILHEAD' with the City of Mukilteo logo. Heavy cedar timber framing visible above the sign.
entries.publicCaption: Big Gulch Trail — North Fork trailhead.

entries.name: mukilteo-speedway-bike-lane-southeast.jpg
entries.description: Mukilteo Speedway bike lane viewed from the sidewalk, with a freshly painted bicycle stencil in the right-most lane in the foreground and a strip-mall commercial frontage with a digital LED billboard on the far side of the road.
entries.publicCaption: Bike lane along Mukilteo Speedway.

entries.name: mukilteo-speedway-bike-lane-south.jpg
entries.description: Mukilteo Speedway looking south from the sidewalk, with a 'RIGHT LANE BICYCLES ONLY' sign in the right foreground and a decorative pedestrian archway (Pacific Northwest / salmon motif) spanning the sidewalk in mid-frame. Multi-lane traffic and storefronts visible beyond.
entries.publicCaption: Bike lane along Mukilteo Speedway.

entries.name: camdens-housing.jpg
entries.description: Walking path through a landscaped courtyard at a multi-family townhome development, with two- and three-story craftsman-style buildings in mixed cream, rose, and blue-gray siding flanking the path.

entries.name: chamber-of-commerce-sign.jpg
entries.description: Exterior sign on the Mukilteo Chamber of Commerce / Visitor Center building, featuring a stylized lighthouse graphic and a 'Mukilteo Lighthouse Festival' banner along the bottom.
entries.publicCaption: Mukilteo Chamber of Commerce.

entries.name: city-hall-deer-5553.jpg
entries.description: Deer on the City Hall grounds.

entries.name: city-hall-deer-5554.jpg
entries.description: Deer on the City Hall grounds.

entries.name: city-hall-leaves-foreground.jpg
entries.description: A single sharp green leaf in the right foreground, with the Mukilteo City Hall building softly blurred behind. Shallow depth of field with bokeh highlights at left.

entries.name: city-hall-bike-rack-entrance.jpg
entries.description: A single parked bicycle in the sculptural bicycle-shaped bike rack outside the Mukilteo City Hall main entrance, with the 'MUKILTEO CITY HALL' wordmark visible on the wood-slat wall.
entries.publicCaption: Bike parking at the Mukilteo City Hall entrance.

entries.name: city-hall-sign.jpg
entries.description: Close-up of the exterior 'MUKILTEO CITY HALL' signage, with a stylized white lighthouse silhouette set between the words on horizontal cedar siding.
entries.publicCaption: Mukilteo City Hall.

entries.name: city-hall-fountain-closeup.jpg
entries.description: Side-on close-up of the basalt-column water feature in the Mukilteo City Hall entry plaza, with the sculptural bicycle bike rack and entry doors blurred in the background.

entries.name: city-hall-bike-rack.jpg
entries.description: Empty sculptural bicycle-shaped bike rack outside the Mukilteo City Hall main entrance, with the 'MUKILTEO CITY HALL' wordmark visible on the wall.

entries.name: city-hall-side-profile-greenery.jpg
entries.description: Side elevation of Mukilteo City Hall showing the row of wood pillars with dark steel brackets, framed by ferns and dense green groundcover with a planted slope and conifer trees in the middle distance.
entries.publicCaption: Mukilteo City Hall.

entries.name: city-hall-side-profile.jpg
entries.description: Side elevation of Mukilteo City Hall, showing the row of wood pillars with steel brackets, a paved walkway with a low bench, and a planted slope with conifer trees beyond.
entries.publicCaption: Mukilteo City Hall.

entries.name: city-hall-fountain-entrance.jpg
entries.description: Mukilteo City Hall entry plaza: the basalt-column water feature in the right foreground, the sculptural bike rack in the middle distance, and the 'MUKILTEO CITY HALL' wordmark on the wall behind.
entries.publicCaption: Mukilteo City Hall.

entries.name: city-hall-dusk.jpg
entries.description: Mukilteo City Hall photographed at blue hour, with interior lights glowing through the glass stair tower and entrance, basalt-column landscape feature in the foreground, and the 'MUKILTEO CITY HALL' wordmark visible on the wall.
entries.publicCaption: Mukilteo City Hall at dusk.

entries.name: mukilteo-commercial-building.jpg
entries.description: Covered sidewalk colonnade of a one-story shopping center with square white columns on brick bases, shed-roof overhang, and a row of storefronts. The nearest visible storefront sign reads 'JEWELERS'; large planters and a sandwich-board poster line the walkway.
entries.notes: 'JEWELERS' signage is visible on the leftmost storefront — could help a local reviewer identify the specific Mukilteo shopping center (e.g., Harbour Pointe Center, Speedway Plaza, or another). Architecture is mid-late 20th century strip-retail with craftsman-influenced columns.

entries.name: community-garden.jpg
entries.description: Close-up of magenta foxglove (Digitalis) flower spikes in bloom, with a small wood-sided shed or cabin and dark conifer forest visible in the soft-focus background.
entries.publicCaption: Foxglove in bloom at a Mukilteo community garden.
entries.notes: Specific community garden not identified; ambiguity acknowledged. Mukilteo has more than one location that could match.

entries.name: goat-trail-rd-sign.jpg
entries.description: Low-angle view of a wooden street-sign post with two green 'GOAT TRAIL RD' street blades (one with a directional arrow), the blank back of a stop sign behind it, and power lines crossing a clear blue sky.
entries.publicCaption: Goat Trail Road street signs.

entries.name: lighthouse-park-restroom.jpg
entries.description: Heavy-timber multi-use pavilion at Lighthouse Park that includes restroom facilities. (Same building / image as lighthouse-park-band-shell.jpg in this batch.)

entries.name: lighthouse-park-roundabout-salmon-sculpture.jpg
entries.description: Tilted cube-shaped metal sculpture at the Lighthouse Park roundabout, with laser-cut salmon and Northwest Coast-inspired design motifs on each face. Photographed in late-afternoon light with the waterfront and Whidbey Island visible in the distance.
entries.publicCaption: Salmon-themed metal sculpture at the Lighthouse Park roundabout.

entries.name: lighthouse-park-boat-launch-san-juan-islands.jpg
entries.description: Golden-hour view down the Lighthouse Park boat-launch pier, with mooring pilings silhouetted against a warm sky and the forested ridgeline of (likely) Whidbey Island in the distance.
entries.publicCaption: Sunset over Puget Sound from the Lighthouse Park boat launch.

entries.name: mukilteo-ferry-terminal-golden-hour.jpg

era.label: old ferry dock — pre-2020 (likely earlier than current terminal opened late 2020)

entries.description: Golden-hour shot of a Washington State ferry berthed at a wood-piling pier at the Mukilteo ferry terminal, with Whidbey Island's wooded silhouette visible across the water at right. Interior cabin lights are just beginning to glow.
entries.publicCaption: Ferry at the Mukilteo terminal at golden hour.
entries.notes: The wood-piling pier in this frame matches the old Mukilteo ferry dock, not the current (opened late 2020) terminal — era of '2023-2025' is likely incorrect; this is most likely an older photo.

entries.name: bronze-animals-on-book-sculpture.jpg
entries.description: Bronze sculpture group of a bear, a rabbit, and a small third animal arranged on an oversized open book that forms the sculpture's base. Installed on a concrete walkway outside a building with cream split-face block walls, dark green window frames, and a covered breezeway.
entries.publicCaption: Bronze literacy-themed sculpture outside the Mukilteo Library.
entries.notes: Location not confirmed from image. Architecture (cream split-face block, dark green window frames, covered breezeway) and the literacy-themed subject matter are consistent with a library or community building entrance — worth checking against the Mukilteo Library exterior before assigning.

entries.name: mukilteo-lighthouse-from-pier.jpg
entries.description: Mukilteo Lighthouse and keeper's quarters viewed across a pebble spit at low tide. The white wood lighthouse with red roof and copper-domed lantern room is centered; a row of weathered pier pilings extends along the shoreline at right. Puget Sound and distant Whidbey Island form the background under a soft overcast sky.
entries.publicCaption: Mukilteo Lighthouse at Lighthouse Park.
entries.notes: Photographed from the beach / pebble spit looking roughly north-northwest toward the lighthouse (the pilings extending to the right read as the public pier seen from its base).

entries.name: losvar-condominiums-from-water.jpg
entries.description: View of a four-story waterfront residential building (Losvar Condominiums per filename) photographed from the water, with continuous balconies, a fronting pier and rip-rap beach, and a forested hillside dotted with houses rising behind.
entries.publicCaption: Mukilteo waterfront near Losvar Condominiums.
entries.notes: Viewpoint: from water, likely boat or ferry (high confidence).

entries.name: mukilteo-station-commuter-parking-broll.jpg
entries.description: B-roll looking down the sidewalk between the Mukilteo Station commuter parking lot (left, with parked cars under young trees) and the Sound Transit Sounder rail tracks (right, behind a wood pergola with metal-mesh fencing). Bright summer daylight.
entries.publicCaption: Sidewalk along the commuter parking at Mukilteo Station.

entries.name: city-hall-broll-deer.jpg
entries.description: A live spotted fawn stands on the driveway in front of the Mukilteo City Hall entrance (address 11930), framed by foreground shrubs.

entries.name: washington-state-ferry-puget-sound.jpg
entries.description: Modern Washington State Ferries vessel (white hull with characteristic dark green trim and twin upper-deck stacks) underway on open water, photographed in profile against a hazy white-grey sky with no shoreline visible. Vessel class not identifiable from this distance/angle.
entries.publicCaption: Washington State Ferry on Puget Sound.
entries.notes: Viewpoint: from water or shoreline (medium confidence).

entries.name: treaty-of-point-elliott-monument-rosehill.jpg
entries.description: Treaty of Point Elliott monument (1855-1930 bronze plaque on a granite stone) photographed at an angle that includes the modern Rosehill Community Center building, landscaped beds, and a wood bench in the background. Same monument as the frontal variant. Camera is facing generally south/southeast.
entries.publicCaption: Treaty of Point Elliott monument near Rosehill Community Center.
entries.notes: View direction: south/southeast (high confidence).

entries.name: treaty-of-point-elliott-monument-frontal.jpg
entries.description: Frontal photograph of the Treaty of Point Elliott monument: a rough-hewn granite stone with a bronze plaque dated 1855-1930. Plaque text commemorates Governor Isaac I. Stevens concluding the treaty in January 1855 with twenty-two tribes, and was erected by a historical society. Concrete steps and a metal railing are visible behind the monument; same monument as treaty-of-point-elliott-monument-rosehill.jpg, photographed from a different angle that excludes the Rosehill Community Center building.
entries.publicCaption: Treaty of Point Elliott monument.
entries.notes: Plaque text visible: dated 1855-1930, references Governor Isaac I. Stevens concluding the treaty in January 1855 with twenty-two tribes, erected by a historical society (full attribution line not legible at this resolution). Frontal crop excludes the Rosehill Community Center building visible in the rosehill variant.

entries.name: totem-park-totem-pole-top.jpg
entries.description: Upward-angle photograph of the upper portion of the weathered totem pole at Mukilteo's Totem Park. The carving features stacked house/longhouse-like structures with windows and shingled roofs, topped by a small bird figure (appears to be a thunderbird or eagle) against a clear blue sky.
entries.publicCaption: Totem pole at Mukilteo Totem Park.

entries.name: apartments-broll-6959.jpg
entries.description: Multi-story apartment / condominium development with a tall wooden clock tower anchoring the foreground, surrounded by autumn-foliage trees and a manicured lawn under a cloudy sky.
entries.notes: Distinctive wooden clock tower with four-sided clock and belfry-style cap may identify the specific development to a local reviewer. Possibly the same or related complex shown in the pilot's 'mf-character-6966' photo, which had similar architectural language.

entries.name: apartments-broll-7000.jpg
entries.description: Quiet residential street lined with two-story cream/white craftsman-style single-family homes with manicured front yards. Stop sign visible at a side intersection.

entries.name: eagle-scout-project-plaque-2012.jpg
entries.description: Close-up of a Boy Scout Eagle Project plaque (Mount Baker Council, November 2012) screwed into the brown-painted wooden frame of an outdoor kiosk or shelter. A partially visible flyer pinned to an adjacent corkboard suggests this is a community/park kiosk.
entries.publicCaption: Boy Scout Eagle Project plaque, 2012.
entries.notes: Plaque includes a personal name (do not surface in any public-facing field). A second flyer is partially visible in the upper-left of the frame and contains two phone numbers and what looks like a pet description — those are also personally-identifying and should be cropped or blurred before any public use.

entries.name: edgewater-beach-ribbon-cutting-2014.jpg
entries.description: Event sign for the Edgewater Beach Pedestrian & Bike Access Ribbon Cutting Event held June 23, 2014, with Puget Sound and Whidbey Island visible in the background. Partner logos include City of Mukilteo, Port of Everett, Sound Transit, and City of Everett.
entries.publicCaption: Edgewater Beach Pedestrian & Bike Access ribbon cutting, 2014.
entries.notes: Event date: 2014-06-23.

entries.name: mukilteo-waterfront-from-hillside.jpg
entries.description: Tree-framed view through summer foliage and a large tree trunk, looking down from a hillside vantage toward the Mukilteo waterfront, a Washington State Ferry mid-crossing on Puget Sound, and the wooded shoreline of Whidbey Island in the distance.
entries.publicCaption: View toward the Mukilteo waterfront and ferry from the hillside.
entries.notes: Viewpoint: from hillside above waterfront (medium confidence).

entries.name: fire-engine.jpg
entries.description: Close-up of the side of a Mukilteo Fire Department engine showing the Maltese-cross 'MUKILTEO FIRE DEPT.' emblem with a Mukilteo Lighthouse central medallion, plus a partially visible 'Snohomish County E-24' apparatus ID decal.
entries.publicCaption: Mukilteo Fire engine.

entries.name: fire.jpg
entries.description: Tight close-up of a fire engine cab front showing 'MUKILTEO' lettering in serif gold/black drop-shadow style across the door, with chrome bumper grille and twin windshield wipers visible.
entries.publicCaption: Mukilteo lettering on a fire engine.
entries.notes: Generic name — could be station, vehicle, signage. Verify.

entries.name: fish-ladder.jpg
entries.description: Circular stepped fish ladder built at the outlet of a moss-covered culvert, with water cascading through the weirs into a forested stream. Designed to allow salmon to ascend the culvert.
entries.publicCaption: A salmon-friendly fish ladder at a Mukilteo stream culvert.

entries.name: flower-6576.jpg

location.name: Barbara Brenner Dobie Memorial Park, 802 Mukilteo Lane, Mukilteo

entries.description: Red-and-white dahlias in bloom at Barbara Brenner Dobie Memorial Park in Mukilteo, with the park sign (featuring the Mukilteo Lighthouse silhouette) in the soft-focus background.
entries.publicCaption: Dahlias bloom at Barbara Brenner Dobie Memorial Park.

entries.name: flower-6583.jpg

location.name: Barbara Brenner Dobie Memorial Park, 802 Mukilteo Lane, Mukilteo

entries.description: Park entrance sign reading 'Barbara Brenner Dobie Memorial Park / Fowler Pear Tree / 802 Mukilteo Lane, Mukilteo, Washington', with a Mukilteo Lighthouse silhouette at the top and dahlias blooming in the foreground.
entries.publicCaption: Barbara Brenner Dobie Memorial Park, home of the Fowler heritage pear tree.

entries.name: for-web-127.jpg
entries.description: Close-up of a magenta-pink flower (appears to be a rockrose / Cistus) with a yellow center and dark maroon basal spots, photographed against soft-focus garden foliage. A small interpretive sign is visible in the background, suggesting a public garden or park.
entries.publicCaption: A rockrose blooms in a Mukilteo public garden.
entries.notes: Suggested rename: 'rockrose-bloom-public-garden.jpg' or 'pink-flower-garden-127.jpg'. The interpretive sign in the background, if readable at higher resolution, may identify the specific garden.

entries.name: for-web-5012.jpg
entries.description: Stone-and-wood entrance monument for the Elliot Pointe neighborhood, with a stylized blue-on-white sail/water emblem above the script 'Elliot Pointe' lettering, set into clipped evergreen hedges.
entries.publicCaption: Entrance to the Elliot Pointe neighborhood.
entries.notes: Rename based on actual content.

entries.name: fowler-grave.jpg
entries.description: Headstone of Capt. N. B. Fowler (died January 22, 1922) in a small bluff-top pioneer cemetery, with a second 19th-century family stone visible at left and Puget Sound and a far shoreline visible beyond a low fence.
entries.publicCaption: Fowler family grave.
entries.notes: Public memorial; historical pioneer family.

entries.name: fowler-pear-tree.jpg

location.name: Barbara Brenner Dobie Memorial Park, 802 Mukilteo Lane, Mukilteo

entries.description: A young (replanted) Fowler heritage pear tree staked in a circular bed with a stacked-stone retaining wall and a small dedication plaque, ringed by rose bushes at Barbara Brenner Dobie Memorial Park (802 Mukilteo Lane). A craftsman-style building with red metal roof is visible at right.
entries.publicCaption: Fowler heritage pear tree.
entries.notes: Tree appears to be a recent replanting/replacement (slender stake-supported sapling), not the original 19th-century heritage pear. Confirm with parks staff whether the original tree was lost and this is a successor planting from cuttings/grafts.

entries.name: harbour-pointe.jpg
entries.description: Landscaped 'Harbour Pointe' monument entrance sign — white panel with dark serif lettering and a small nautical graphic — set in a mowed median with low hedges and seasonal flower plantings, framed by mature trees.
entries.publicCaption: The Harbour Pointe neighborhood entrance.

entries.name: historic-flight-6382.jpg
entries.description: Close-up of the polished chrome spinner, three-blade propeller, and radial engine of a red vintage aircraft. Cursive script and a small winged emblem are visible on the cowling. Overcast sky.
entries.notes: Historic Flight Foundation has since relocated; verify currency before public use.

entries.name: historic-flight-6385.jpg
entries.description: Side view of the open cockpit and forward fuselage of a vintage red-and-white tandem-seat aircraft with a polished aluminum nose, two-blade propeller, and a panel of analog flight instruments visible inside the canopy.

entries.name: historic-flight-6394.jpg
entries.description: Three-quarter front view of a polished bare-aluminum military trainer aircraft (consistent with a North American T-6 Texan / SNJ) with a black propeller, large radial engine, and 'U.S.' lettering on the fuselage. Tarmac setting with conifers behind and what looks like an inflatable from a public event partly visible behind the wing.

entries.name: island-view-ridge.jpg
entries.description: 'Island View Ridge' neighborhood entrance monument — hand-painted-style sign panel under a wood-beam pergola with stacked-stone columns and vine drape, framed by manicured shrubs.
entries.publicCaption: The Island View Ridge neighborhood entrance.

entries.name: ivars-5129.jpg
entries.description: Twilight photo of the Ivar's reader-board sign: top panel reads 'Where Clams & Culture Meet!' (the Ivar's tagline); reader board below shows 'FRESH COPPER RIVER SALMON'; a 'Fresh Seafood' neon sign glows beneath. Tan wood-sided building exterior; deep blue dusk sky.
entries.publicCaption: Copper River salmon on the menu at Ivar's, photographed at dusk.

entries.name: ivars-5131.jpg
entries.description: Close-up of the Ivar's neon script wordmark on a wood-sided exterior, with a 'MUKILTEO LANDING 1926' plaque visible above.
entries.publicCaption: Ivar's at Mukilteo Landing.

entries.name: japanese-gulch-sunset.jpg
entries.description: Warm golden sunset light filtering through a gap in dense conifers, illuminating ferns and understory in the center of the frame against tall dark trunks — a 'god rays' effect through the forest.
entries.publicCaption: Sunset at Japanese Gulch.

entries.name: japanese-gulch-trail.jpg
entries.description: Soft dirt walking trail through sun-dappled second-growth deciduous forest, with bright green understory (sword fern, salmonberry) lining both sides and tall slender alder/maple trunks forming the canopy.
entries.publicCaption: A walking trail through Japanese Gulch.

entries.name: japanese-gulch-5755.jpg
entries.description: Looking up into a dense second-growth deciduous forest canopy with sunlight filtering through bright green leaves; multi-trunked maples or alders dominate the frame, with conifers at right.
entries.publicCaption: Sunlight through the canopy in Japanese Gulch.

entries.name: japanese-gulch-5821.jpg
entries.description: Narrow dirt trail through a Pacific Northwest conifer forest, with the rough red-brown bark of a large western redcedar (likely) dominating the foreground at right; soft-focus ferns and conifer trunks recede into the background.
entries.publicCaption: A trail through the cedars in Japanese Gulch.

entries.name: legion-of-boom.jpg
entries.description: Group of approximately 13 people in Seattle Seahawks jerseys (numbers 12 and 29 visible) posing outside the entrance to Mukilteo City Hall, holding a large hand-painted 'LEGION OF BOOM' banner. Likely a Blue Friday / Seahawks playoff-season staff or officials photo.
entries.publicCaption: Mukilteo staff show their Seahawks pride at City Hall.
entries.notes: Group photo at Mukilteo City Hall entrance with a hand-painted 'Legion of Boom' banner. Trademark note remains relevant — 'Legion of Boom' is associated with the Seattle Seahawks; consider whether using it in public-facing captions creates any concern, or simply caption as 'Seahawks pride at City Hall' to sidestep the issue. Identifiable individuals appear in the photo — verify image-release status before public use.

entries.name: library-5357.jpg
entries.description: Mukilteo Library entrance monument sign at 4675, reading 'MUKILTEO LIBRARY' in green lettering on a stone-and-block monument, with a small lighthouse icon inset.
entries.publicCaption: Mukilteo Library entrance sign.

entries.name: library-5365.jpg
entries.description: Bronze sculpture group of a child reaching toward a standing bear cub, with an otter beside them, on a concrete pad outside the Mukilteo Library. Photographed against a backdrop of landscaping shrubs.
entries.publicCaption: Bronze sculpture at the Mukilteo Library.

entries.name: library-5368.jpg
entries.description: Low-angle artistic shot of the bronze sculpture group at the Mukilteo Library, framed through a third sculpture figure (appears to be a wolf or coyote) in the soft-focus foreground, with a bear cub and children figures sharp in the middle distance. The library building's exterior — cream split-face block walls with green-framed windows — is visible behind.
entries.publicCaption: Bronze sculpture group outside the Mukilteo Library.

entries.name: lighthouse-park-6690.jpg
entries.description: Elevated overview of the Mukilteo Light complex from above the Lighthouse Park parking lot, looking north across Puget Sound to Whidbey Island. Lighthouse tower, keeper's quarters, picnic shelter, and full parking lot visible on a sunny day.
entries.publicCaption: Mukilteo's Lighthouse Park and Puget Sound waterfront.

entries.name: lighthouse-park-6696.jpg
entries.description: Elevated view of Lighthouse Park looking north over the BNSF railroad tracks toward the lighthouse and Puget Sound. Shot from a viewpoint higher and further south than the other elevated park views in this set, with the tracks and a white wooden fence framing the foreground.
entries.publicCaption: Lighthouse Park and the BNSF rail line along Mukilteo's waterfront.

entries.name: lighthouse-park-6698.jpg
entries.description: Elevated tighter-frame view of the Mukilteo Light complex and Lighthouse Park parking lot, looking north across Puget Sound to Whidbey Island. Very similar viewpoint to lighthouse-park-6690 but tighter on the lighthouse buildings.
entries.publicCaption: Lighthouse Park.

entries.name: lighthouse-park-6737.jpg
entries.description: Elevated view of the Mukilteo Light and Lighthouse Park with a Washington State Ferry crossing Puget Sound in the middle distance, headed between Mukilteo and Clinton (Whidbey Island).
entries.publicCaption: A Washington State Ferry crosses Puget Sound near Mukilteo's Lighthouse Park.

entries.name: lighthouse-closeup.jpg
entries.description: Wide environmental view of the Mukilteo Light and keeper's quarters at low tide, photographed from the beach with a weathered wooden pier and pilings in the foreground. Whidbey Island visible in the distance under overcast skies.
entries.publicCaption: The Mukilteo Light at low tide, viewed from the beach.
entries.notes: Confirm framing/quality before promoting to hero use.

entries.name: losvar.jpg
entries.description: Dusk view from a waterfront pier of the Losvar building (a long four-story residential structure with continuous balconies). Warm interior lights glow against a fading blue sky; the pier railing leads the eye toward the building.
entries.publicCaption: Losvar at dusk on the Mukilteo waterfront.
entries.notes: Building shape and balcony pattern match losvar-condominiums-from-water.jpg in this batch — same building photographed from opposite sides (water-side daytime vs. pier-side dusk). Treat as the same subject (Losvar Condominiums).

entries.name: mf-character-6966.jpg
entries.description: Wooden clock tower with a four-sided clock and copper-shingled cap, set in a brick paver plaza surrounded by craftsman-style buildings and autumn trees. Likely the centerpiece of a planned 'village center' style development.
entries.publicCaption: Clock tower at a Mukilteo town-center plaza.
entries.notes: 'MF' in the original filename appears to be a photographer/Lightroom collection prefix, not a subject. Visible content is a clock tower in a planned town-center style plaza — possibly Harbour Pointe Boulevard's village center / a Smith Brothers / Old Town / Harbour Pointe commercial node. Confirm location with a local reviewer before captioning.

entries.name: mf-character-6968.jpg
entries.description: Row of craftsman-style townhomes with green siding, tapered white porch columns, and a tree-lined sidewalk in autumn light. Setting reads as a planned multifamily / town-center development.
entries.notes: 'MF' in the original filename is almost certainly 'Multi-Family' — confirmed by visual content of mf-character-6968 (townhome row) and mf-character-6981 (townhome cluster), and consistent with the pilot's mf-character-6966 (clock tower in a multi-family town-center plaza). Treat as a photographer/Lightroom collection prefix for multifamily-housing photos.

entries.name: mf-character-6981.jpg
entries.description: Cluster of three-story craftsman-style townhomes with mixed-color siding, peaked gables and decorative trusses, arranged around a landscaped courtyard with a winding walking path, manicured lawn, and bollard lights.
entries.notes: Same 'MF' = 'Multi-Family' interpretation as mf-character-6968. This and 6968 appear to be from the same townhome development photographed from different angles (similar siding palette, trim style, landscaping). Possibly the same shoot day.

entries.name: morris-grave.jpg
entries.description: Weathered marble headstone of Morris H. Frost (died January 1882, aged 70, 'a native of New York') in a grassy clearing under mature trees. The stone bears a Masonic square-and-compass emblem. A split-rail fence and a glimpse of water are visible in the background — consistent with a small waterfront-bluff pioneer cemetery.
entries.publicCaption: Headstone of Morris H. Frost (1812-1882) in Mukilteo's Pioneer Cemetery.
entries.notes: Public historical marker in a pioneer cemetery; subject (Morris H. Frost, d. 1882) is long deceased so no privacy concern. Masonic emblem on the stone may be of interest. Verify Pioneer Cemetery location with local source — visible waterfront glimpse is consistent with the bluff-side Mukilteo Pioneer Cemetery.

entries.name: mukilteo-station-5560.jpg
entries.description: Close-up of the 'Mukilteo Station' identifier sign at the Sound Transit Sounder commuter rail station, framed by ornamental grasses and orange daylilies in the foreground and a wooden pergola in the background.
entries.publicCaption: The Mukilteo Station identifier sign on the Sound Transit Sounder line.

entries.name: mukilteo-station-5563.jpg
entries.description: Close-up of the 'Mukilteo Station' identifier sign at the Sound Transit Sounder commuter rail station, framed by ornamental grasses and a wooden pergola. Near-duplicate of mukilteo-station-5560.jpg with a slightly different camera angle.
entries.publicCaption: Mukilteo Station.

entries.name: oil-dock.jpg
entries.description: View of a long industrial pier/dock structure extending into the water from the Mukilteo waterfront, with Whidbey Island visible across Possession Sound. Rocky beach and driftwood in the foreground.
entries.publicCaption: The Mukilteo oil dock and waterfront, looking across Possession Sound toward Whidbey Island.

entries.name: pioneer-cemetery-6521.jpg
entries.description: Entrance sign for Pioneer Cemetery (513 Webster Street, Mukilteo) — carved teal-and-cream wooden sign at the edge of a lawn, with a glimpse of Puget Sound and distant island visible past the hedge.
entries.publicCaption: The entrance sign at Pioneer Cemetery on Webster Street.

entries.name: pioneer-cemetery-6549.jpg
entries.description: Interior view of Pioneer Cemetery showing two upright historic headstones on a lawn beneath mature maple trees, with the cemetery's entrance sign visible at right and a small wood-sided shelter in the background.
entries.publicCaption: Pioneer Cemetery.

entries.name: police-car-5842.jpg
entries.description: Mukilteo Police patrol vehicle — a black-and-white Ford Taurus Police Interceptor with 'POLICE / CITY OF MUKILTEO' door decals and the Mukilteo Lighthouse graphic, parked next to event tents (likely a community event setting).
entries.publicCaption: Mukilteo Police patrol vehicle.

entries.name: police-car-5843.jpg
entries.description: Close-up of a Mukilteo Police vehicle door showing the 'POLICE / CITY OF MUKILTEO' decal and Mukilteo Lighthouse silhouette graphic.
entries.publicCaption: Mukilteo Police patrol vehicle.

entries.name: police-car-5847.jpg
entries.description: Mukilteo Police SUV (appears to be a Ford Police Interceptor Utility / Explorer) with 'POLICE / CITY OF MUKILTEO' door decals and the Mukilteo Lighthouse graphic, parked outdoors on a sunny day.
entries.publicCaption: Mukilteo Police patrol vehicle.

entries.name: pet-waste-station.jpg
entries.description: Green-and-cream sign mounted on a wooden rail fence reading 'If Your Dog Poops, Please Scoop... It's the Right Thing to Do!' with a pictogram of a person cleaning up after a leashed dog. Park or trail setting.
entries.publicCaption: Pet etiquette signage at a Mukilteo park.
entries.notes: Renamed from 'Poop and Scoop' to a more public-appropriate label.

entries.name: post-office.jpg
entries.description: United States Post Office in Mukilteo, Washington (ZIP 98275, building number 8060) — single-story tan-sided building with gabled roof, USPS eagle plaque, and a US flag flying in the foreground.
entries.publicCaption: The United States Post Office in Mukilteo (ZIP 98275).

entries.name: public-works.jpg
entries.description: Concrete monument sign reading 'MUKILTEO PUBLIC WORKS' in front of the city's Public Works building, with landscaping (Japanese maple, shrubs) and an accessible-parking sign visible.
entries.publicCaption: Mukilteo Public Works.
entries.notes: Expanded 'PW' abbreviation to full name.

entries.name: public-meeting-5271.jpg

location.name: City of Mukilteo (likely City Hall / Council Chamber lobby)

entries.description: A 'City of Mukilteo Planning 101: The Confusing World of Land Use Regulations & Planning' open house in what appears to be the council chamber lobby. A staff member (woman in coral top) stands behind a sign-in / handout table; an easel-mounted poster and a projected presentation slide both display the event title. Framed land-use maps line the wall behind.
entries.publicCaption: Public meeting.
entries.notes: One identifiable staff member (woman in coral top) clearly visible in frame and posed for camera. Confirm image release before public use. Event identified from poster as 'Planning 101' open house.

entries.name: public-meeting-7730.jpg
entries.description: A workshop-style public engagement session: a participant (man in dark grey polo with embroidered crest) is seated at a table with a large land-use / zoning map, photos, and craft supplies (scissors, glue stick), engaged in what looks like a community-input or visioning exercise. Other participants are partially visible.
entries.publicCaption: Public meeting.
entries.notes: One clearly identifiable participant (man in dark grey polo) is the dominant subject. Embroidered crest on his shirt may be identifying (appears to read '1991' with a stylized graphic). Two additional partially-visible participants in background. Confirm image release before public use.

entries.name: public-notices.jpg

location.name: Mukilteo, WA (likely City Hall or Public Works exterior)

entries.description: Blue 'CITY OF MUKILTEO / PUBLIC NOTICES' awning with the Mukilteo Lighthouse graphic, mounted on a cinderblock exterior wall (likely a public-facing wall of a city facility used to post legal/public notices).
entries.publicCaption: City of Mukilteo public notices signage.

entries.name: railroad.jpg
entries.description: Single-track railroad line running along the Mukilteo waterfront between a rocky beach (left) and a wooded bluff (right), with Puget Sound and the far shore visible. Bare deciduous branches and low-angle light suggest a winter or early-spring afternoon.
entries.publicCaption: The railroad line tracing the Mukilteo waterfront.

entries.name: red-cup-coffee.jpg
entries.description: Two-story dark-sided building housing the Red Cup coffee shop, with a corner patio under red umbrellas, mature landscaping, and the shop's red signage on both visible elevations.
entries.publicCaption: Red Cup.

entries.name: rosehill-community-center.jpg
entries.description: View of the Rosehill Community Center campus showing a 1928-dated decorative concrete bench/balustrade in the foreground, with the historic red-clapboard schoolhouse-style building joined to the modern community-center addition behind.
entries.publicCaption: Rosehill Community Center.
entries.notes: Subject is the Rosehill Community Center campus from the side that shows the 1928 commemorative bench and the historic red-clapboard schoolhouse-style building (likely a preserved/relocated element of the original 1928 Rose Hill School). The modern community-center addition is visible behind. Confirm whether the red building is the relocated historic schoolhouse and whether the 1928 bench has its own dedication name worth captioning.

entries.name: sidewalk.jpg
entries.description: Concrete sidewalk along a quiet residential street, separated from the road by a grass strip and ornamental street trees, with two-story homes and mature landscaping at left.

entries.name: sound-transit-station.jpg
entries.description: Pedestrian walkway running between the Mukilteo Sound Transit station parking lot and the trackside fence (with wood pergola overhead), looking down the platform-adjacent path toward the curving rails.
entries.publicCaption: Sound Transit at Mukilteo.
entries.notes: View is the parking-lot-side walkway alongside the tracks (wood pergola overhead). Compare to mukilteo-station-* in batch 7 to confirm it is a complementary angle (not a duplicate). The wood pergola and wire-mesh fence styling should match.

entries.name: speed-safety.jpg
entries.description: Close-up of a residential 'Please drive carefully for our children's sake — Speed Limit 25 MPH' street sign mounted on a wooden post, against an overcast sky.
entries.publicCaption: A neighborhood speed-limit sign reminds drivers to slow down for children.
entries.notes: Source filename had a typo ('Saftey') and trailing dash; corrected.

entries.name: sr-525.jpg
entries.description: Overlook view from the top of the Mukilteo Speedway (SR 525) descending into Old Town, with a green 'OLD TOWN' wayfinder sign in the foreground, the ferry terminal and a docked Washington State Ferry visible below, and Whidbey Island across Possession Sound.
entries.publicCaption: The Mukilteo Speedway (SR 525) descends into Old Town and the ferry terminal, with Whidbey Island beyond.

entries.name: stacy-memorial-clock.jpg
entries.description: The Stacy Shown Watchmaker Jeweler memorial street clock — an ornate cast-iron pedestal clock with twin globe-lamp clusters flanking the dial — a public landmark in Old Town Mukilteo.
entries.publicCaption: Stacy memorial clock.
entries.notes: Dial reads 'Stacy Shown — Watchmaker Jeweler'. Confirm whether the formal name of the landmark is 'Stacy Shown Memorial Clock' (or similar) and whether 'Stacy Clock' in the manifest is just shorthand. Distinct from the modern wooden clock tower seen in batch 5/8 (mf-character-6966.jpg) — that one is a different planned-development clock tower.

entries.name: stormwater-ditch.jpg
entries.description: Low-angle (water-level) view of a small roadside stormwater ditch with active runoff, shot looking up the channel toward a yellow 'Hidden Driveway / Speed 25' warning sign on a wooden post.
entries.publicCaption: Stormwater drainage.

entries.name: street-1.jpg
entries.description: Two-lane curving parkway-style road through a dense forest of conifers and bare deciduous trees in winter, with a yellow 'BUMP' warning sign at right and a distant glimpse of Possession Sound through the trees.
entries.notes: Source name had trailing dash; assigned street-1 to disambiguate from -2/-3.

entries.name: street-2.jpg
entries.description: Residential street splitting around a tree-planted center median in autumn, with maroon-leaved ornamental trees on the median and quiet homes lining both sides under overcast light.

entries.name: street-3.jpg
entries.description: Residential street with paired bare-branch street trees, curbed sidewalks, and city refuse/recycle bins at the curb on a sunny late-winter day.

entries.name: street-6963.jpg
entries.description: Mixed-use 'main street' style commercial corner with a three-story building (ground-floor storefronts including a pizza restaurant with sidewalk seating), brick crosswalks, and ornamental autumn trees lining the sidewalk.
entries.notes: Architectural style (cream stucco, white pilasters, sloped roof, brick crosswalks, planned street-tree program) is consistent with the same town-center development seen in mf-character-6966.jpg (clock tower) from an earlier batch. Worth confirming whether this is the Harbour Pointe village center, a Smith Brothers area development, or another specific planned commercial node.

entries.name: stormwater-7998.jpg
entries.description: Roadside stormwater drainage swale running alongside a multi-lane arterial road in winter, with traffic-signal mast-arms and a strip-mall commercial center (golden-arches signage visible) in the background.
entries.notes: Source name typo ('Stromwater') corrected to 'stormwater'.

entries.name: sunset.jpg
entries.description: Sunset over Puget Sound photographed from a beach, with silhouetted dune grass in the foreground and a long tree-covered landmass (consistent with Whidbey Island) on the horizon.
entries.publicCaption: Sunset over Puget Sound from the Mukilteo shoreline.

entries.name: trees.jpg
entries.description: Upward-angle b-roll of bright lime-green deciduous tree canopy against a pale-blue sky; reads as spring or early summer.

entries.name: wastewater-treatment-plant.jpg
entries.description: Covered circular treatment basin (likely a clarifier or aeration tank) at a wastewater treatment plant — blue steel column structure supporting a standing-seam metal roof with a central vent cupola, with a second similar structure visible at left and a conifer treeline in the background.
entries.publicCaption: Wastewater Treatment Plant.

entries.name: waterfront.jpg
entries.description: Mid-distance view of the Mukilteo waterfront from the water, showing a multistory shoreline condominium/hotel building, what appears to be a small white red-roofed structure (possibly the Mukilteo Lighthouse or a related building), and a wooded hillside rising behind the shore with houses visible among the evergreens. Calm water foreground, cloudy sky.
entries.publicCaption: Mukilteo waterfront.

entries.name: ymca.jpg
entries.description: Exterior of the Harbour Pointe YMCA building: gray split-face block walls with a 'the Y' logo and address '10601' visible on the wall, a covered timber-frame entrance breezeway, and a U.S. flag on a tall flagpole. Photographed on a sunny day with sunburst flare in the upper frame.
entries.publicCaption: Harbour Pointe YMCA.
entries.notes: Visible address number '10601' on the wall — consistent with the Harbour Pointe YMCA address (10601 47th Pl W, Mukilteo). 'the Y' branding is the post-2010 YMCA logo, supporting a modern (2010s+) era estimate.

entries.name: rosehill-community-center-patio-north.jpg
entries.description: Outdoor patio at the Rosehill Community Center under a wood-clad eave, with wrought-iron tables and chairs facing a manicured lawn and a backdrop of autumn-colored trees and conifers.
entries.publicCaption: Rosehill Community Center patio.
entries.notes: Large file (~18MB). May want to web-optimize before publishing.

entries.name: mukilteo-fire-engine-community-event-1970s.jpg
entries.description: Head-on view of a red Mukilteo fire engine ('MUKILTEO' lettered across the front with fleur-de-lis emblems) parked on a street, with two white Mukilteo Fire Dept. emergency vehicles visible at left and a small group of onlookers at right. Likely a community event or open-house gathering; era appears to be 1980s based on vehicle styling.
entries.publicCaption: Mukilteo Fire Department engine on display at a community event, 1980s.
entries.notes: Era is a visual estimate; verify if possible.

entries.name: mukilteo-fire-department-vintage-rescue-vehicle.jpg
entries.description: Color snapshot (re-photographed from an older print — visible glare and scratches) of a yellow vintage panel van / sedan-delivery vehicle outfitted for the Mukilteo Fire Department. Chrome grille and trim, twin chrome warning lights on the front fenders, and a red roof beacon. A circular red 'FIRE DEPT / [firefighter figure] / MUKILTEO' logo is visible on the rear quarter panel. The grille/trim styling is more consistent with a 1953-1955 Packard or Pontiac sedan-delivery body than a Chevrolet — let a vintage car expert confirm before captioning specifically.
entries.publicCaption: Mukilteo Fire Department historical vehicle.
entries.notes: Image is a re-photograph of an older print (visible glare/scratches), so resolution is limited. Vehicle make/model needs a vintage-vehicle expert — front-end chrome looks more Packard/Pontiac than Chevrolet. The 'FIRE DEPT MUKILTEO' badge confirms department ownership.

entries.name: rosehill-community-center-south-exterior.jpg
entries.description: Exterior of the Rosehill Community Center showing the long glass-walled south elevation under a heavy timber pergola, with red wood siding at the corner and landscaped beds of horsetails and grasses along the walkway.
entries.publicCaption: Rosehill Community Center.
entries.notes: Source is a 45MB TIF; copy step uses ImageMagick to convert to JPG (quality 90).

entries.name: mukilteo-aerial-from-water-1936.jpg

location.name: Mukilteo, WA — view from the water

entries.description: Aerial / oblique photograph of Mukilteo's waterfront and uphill town fabric in 1936, showing the long shoreline dock, a curving beach, the railroad along the shore, and streets cut into a forested slope. Published in 1951 with the printed caption '"MUKILTEO — 15 YEARS AGO — 1936-1951"'.
entries.publicCaption: Mukilteo from the air, 1936 (from a 1951 retrospective print).
entries.notes: Source date confirmed in the printed caption visible at the bottom of the image: '"MUKILTEO — 15 YEARS AGO — 1936-1951"'. Photo content is 1936; the print is from 1951.

entries.name: douglas-dc3-american-airlines-historical-livery.jpg
entries.description: Polished aluminum Douglas DC-3 in flight, painted in historical Pan American Airways System livery (with a small American flag forward of the cabin windows).
entries.publicCaption: Douglas DC-3 in Pan American Airways historical livery.
entries.notes: Almost certainly photographed at the Historic Flight Foundation when it was in Mukilteo.

entries.name: lighthouse-park-early-historical.jpg
entries.description: Historical photograph of the Mukilteo Light and keeper's quarters viewed from the water, with American flag flying, a wooden pier extending into the foreground, and the early Mukilteo hillside (sparsely settled) climbing behind. Lighthouse profile matches the present-day Mukilteo Light.
entries.publicCaption: Lighthouse Park, early 20th century.
entries.notes: Source filename '2000.32' looks like an accession number, not a year. Confirm date with the source archive.

entries.name: mukilteo-store-vintage.jpg
entries.description: Vintage real-photo postcard of historic Mukilteo, showing (left to right) a residence, 'A. LEMP'S HOTEL' with a 'GROCERY STORE' entrance, and the 'MUKILTEO STORE / N. J. SMITH Prop' building on a dirt road with wooden boardwalks. A small figure stands in a doorway under the Mukilteo Store balcony; a chicken is visible in the foreground.
entries.publicCaption: Historic Mukilteo: A. Lemp's Hotel and the Mukilteo Store (N. J. Smith, proprietor), early 1900s.
entries.notes: '2000.67' is almost certainly a museum accession number (year.item format), not a photo date. Visible signage names: A. Lemp (hotel), N. J. Smith (Mukilteo Store proprietor) — both should be checkable against Mukilteo Historical Society records to date the photo more precisely.

entries.name: mukilteo-ferry-terminal-vashon-ferry.jpg
entries.description: Faded color photograph (looks like a 1970s-80s scanned print or slide) of a green-and-white Washington State ferry — name on bow consistent with 'VASHON' — departing the old Mukilteo ferry dock. The Mukilteo Lighthouse and its keeper's cottage are clearly visible along the shore at left, with a rip-rap revetment running down the beach.
entries.publicCaption: Earlier Washington State ferry at Mukilteo.
entries.notes: Confirm vessel name — likely the MV Vashon. '2000.779' is accession-style.

entries.name: mukilteo-fire-department-vintage-truck.jpg
subjects: Snohomish County Fire Protection District No. 2
entries.description: Sepia-toned profile photo of a vintage cab-over-engine fire truck (Ford COE styling, late 1930s / early 1940s) lettered '"MUKILTEO"' on the door and 'SNOHOMISH COUNTY FIRE PROTECTION DISTRICT NO. 2' on the tank panel. Equipped with ladders, hose connections, gauges, bell, and spotlight.
entries.publicCaption: Mukilteo Fire Department historical truck.

entries.name: island-flyer-boat-docked.jpg
entries.description: Vintage black-and-white photograph of the small wooden vessel ISLAND FLYER (name visible on the transom) moored against a piling. A man in workwear and cap stands atop the wheelhouse beside the smokestack; open water and a distant forested shoreline are visible at left.
entries.publicCaption: The Island Flyer, docked.
entries.notes: Vessel name 'ISLAND FLYER' is visually confirmed from the transom. Location remains unverified — distant low shoreline at left could be Whidbey Island as seen from Mukilteo, but is not distinctive enough to confirm. One identifiable individual (man on the wheelhouse) is in the frame; vintage photo, but image-release status should still be considered before public use.

entries.name: native-american-dedication-ceremony-1960s.jpg
entries.description: Black-and-white photograph of an outdoor dedication ceremony. A woman in elaborate Native regalia (long fringed / beaded dress, beaded headband, long earrings) stands at a microphone with one arm raised; seated near her are a man in a feathered ceremonial headdress, another figure in feathered regalia, and a man in a dark suit with thick-rimmed glasses next to a smiling young woman. A large floral arrangement and pleated bunting decorate the stage; the out-of-focus background shows tents and trees consistent with an outdoor fair or festival setting.
entries.publicCaption: Speakers at a dedication ceremony in Mukilteo, late 1950s to mid 1960s.
entries.notes: Confirm tribal affiliation and identities before captioning. Mukilteo is the Treaty of Point Elliott site, so Coast Salish peoples (including Tulalip, Snohomish, and others) are plausible — but the feathered headdress visible on one seated figure is more typically associated with Plains-style regalia, which could indicate a guest/performer from another nation, a Pan-Indian style adopted for ceremonial appearances, or a misreading on my part. Do not assert tribal affiliation without confirmation from the historical society or affiliated tribes.

entries.name: mukilteo-deep-water-harbor-parade.jpg
entries.description: Historical parade photograph showing Mukilteo civic-promotion delegates in an urban street, carrying two banners: 'MUKILTEO / A / DEEP WATER / HARBOR' and 'MUKILTEOS [sic] / PAY ROLE / $30,000'. A Pantages Theater sign is visible on a building at right; tall masonry commercial buildings, horse-drawn vehicles, formal dress (boater hats, long dresses with elaborate hats), and brick paving date this to the early 1900s. The Pantages and the urban scale strongly suggest this is downtown Seattle, not Mukilteo.
entries.publicCaption: Mukilteo deep-water-harbor campaign delegates parade in (likely) Seattle, circa 1905-1915. Note: 'Mukilteos' on the smaller banner appears to be a period spelling/typo.
entries.notes: Pantages Theater sign visible at right anchors this to a city with a Pantages — most plausibly downtown Seattle (Pantages on 2nd Ave, ~1907-1915). The 'MUKILTEOS PAY ROLE $30,000' banner is part of a civic-promotion campaign arguing for federal/state investment in a Mukilteo deep-water harbor. 'Pay role' = period spelling of payroll.

entries.name: mukilteo-ferry-aerial-2006.jpg
entries.description: True aerial photo (taken June 27, 2006 at 2:35 PM per overlaid timestamp) showing a Washington State ferry departing the old Mukilteo ferry terminal. The dock, terminal buildings, full parking lots, the BNSF mainline along the shore, and the Mukilteo Speedway climbing into town are all visible. A second smaller vessel is near the dock.
entries.publicCaption: Washington State ferry departing the old Mukilteo ferry dock, 2006.
entries.notes: 'Old' dock — replaced by the current terminal that opened in late 2020.

entries.name: mukilteo-everett-stages-bus-barn.jpg
entries.description: Sepia-toned vintage photo of three Mukilteo-Everett Stages coaches parked in front of the bus barn. Large painted 'MUKILTEO – EVERETT STAGES' signage spans the building wall; the lead coach (numbered 27) shows a 'MUKILTEO–EVERETT' destination sign. A faint date stamp at the bottom of the print appears to read August of a 195x year.
entries.publicCaption: Mukilteo-Everett Stages bus barn, mid-20th century.
entries.notes: Era is a visual estimate.

entries.name: mukilteo-train-station-steam-locomotive.jpg
entries.description: Black-and-white historical photograph of a steam locomotive arriving at the Mukilteo depot. A 'Great Northern Express Co.' sign is visible on the depot wall, and a Wabash-lettered boxcar sits on a side track. Several men in early-1900s clothing wait on the platform with a hand cart of freight.
entries.publicCaption: Steam locomotive at the old Mukilteo station.
entries.notes: No archival source / historical-society reference available. Visible "Great Northern Express Co." signage anchors the railroad context. Historical figures visible in frame; given the early-1900s era they are presumed public-domain and image releases are not required.

operations: resize long edge to max 2000px (do not upscale)
optimization.notes: In-repo files are web-optimized derivatives. Source originals are referenced per-entry by originalFilename and originalSha256 for audit/reverse-lookup; full source paths live only in the local (untracked) source map.
optimization.metadataPolicy: EXIF stripped from web derivatives in this repo. Original metadata preserved only in source originals (referenced by originalSha256).
